summary: Set 032 Session 2 (final): drafted Set 033 implementation spec.md from the 6 locked verdicts (H1-H4, OQ1, OQ2) Session 1 closed. 6-session implementation plan covers S1 state machine + start_session refactor (H1+H3+H4+OQ1), S2 marker retirement + resolver refactor + banner removal (H2), S3 UI rename + Command Palette Release Check-Out + Claude hook refactor to invoker (H1), S4 Layer-3 Playwright coverage of multi-set rendering + refusal error content + force-override + release command + re-attach, S5 queueing/polling using H4 identity predicate, S6 cross-tier close_session check-in (Full + Lightweight) + canonical doc updates (schema, close-out, workflow) + cross-repo CLAUDE.md notification + PyPI + Marketplace release. Cross-reviewed via Gemini Pro (route_spec_review.py, $0.020); verdict APPROVE-WITH-SUGGESTIONS with two non-blocking refinements (S6 close-session idempotence + R7 listInProgressSets() performance risk); both applied. audit-input README flipped from 'audit resolved' to 'audit-then-spec cycle complete' with explicit Set 033 spec pointer (replacing the 'placeholder spec currently' note); cost record extended with Session 1 verification + Session 2 cross-review (~$0.079 total architectural-decision spend across all rounds). Round-A verification (gemini-pro $0.038) VERIFIED with no must-fix: all 6 verdicts traced with quoted citations, both cross-review suggestions confirmed applied, per-session sequencing internally consistent, supporting artifacts honest, no load-bearing omissions. Round B not required. Set 032 closes inside its $0.10-$0.40 forecast band; ~$0.90 of $1.00 NTE headroom remains. Set 032 completes the audit-then-spec cycle; Set 033 implementation can execute against the cross-reviewed spec as-is. Set 033 session-state.json titles updated to align with the authored spec (no placeholder text remains).
